item.title: $40,000 CD vs. $40,000 high-yield savings account vs. $40,000 money market account: Here's which will earn more now
item.description: Interest earnings on a $40,000 deposit will vary, sometimes significantly, depending on the account type.

item.title: Senate passes another short-term FISA extension, hours before deadline
item.description: The Senate on Thursday passed an extension of a key surveillance authority that allows U.S. intelligence agencies to spy on foreigners without a warrant, the latest in a back-and-forth with the House over the expiring program.

item.title: Louisiana suspends House primaries for May 16 elections
item.description: Louisiana Secretary of State Nancy Landry said Thursday that the state will suspend its May 16 House primaries in the wake of the Supreme Court striking down​ the state's Congressional map.

item.title: Iran war pushes oil prices to 4-year high as Hegseth faces lawmakers
item.description: Oil prices hit a 4-year high as Axios reports Trump will hear new options to try to break the Strait of Hormuz standoff with Iran with a new wave of attacks.

item.title: Senate rejects 6th Iran war powers resolution ahead of 60-day deadline
item.description: The Senate rejected Democrats' sixth attempt to limit President Trump's authority to wage war on Iran.

item.title: Watch: Warren presses Hegseth about insider trading on Iran war developments
item.description: Democratic Sen. Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts pressed Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth about regulations in the Pentagon on dealing with insider trading with developments on the Iran war on prediction markets. Hegseth largely sidestepped the question, saying the department is "focused on our mission on executing for the American people."

item.title: Spotify's new badge identifies human artists as AI music floods include
item.description: As AI-generated music spreads, Spotify says it wants to help users "trust the authenticity" of what they're listening to.

item.title: Watch: Kaine questions Caine about legal justification of U.S. strikes on alleged drug boats
item.description: Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine questioned chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Gen. Dan Caine about legal justification of U.S. strikes on alleged drug boats in the Caribbean Sea, and then asked Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth about the Iran war reaching the 60-day deadline set by the War Powers Resolution of 1973. Hegseth claimed the ceasefire paused the clock for the deadline.

item.title: 3 mortgage rate questions borrowers should ask after the Fed rate pause
item.description: Another Fed rate pause has left mortgage borrowers with more questions than answers. Here are three to contemplate now.

item.title: As Iran war nears key 60-day deadline, Congress and Trump face choices
item.description: The War Powers Resolution sets deadlines for the president to end hostilities without congressional approval.

item.title: Texas mother of four now free after more than a month in ICE detention
item.description: A legal U.S. resident and mother of four children is now free after ICE held her in an immigration detention facility for more than a month. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul reports.

item.title: Johnson touts passage of DHS funding bill despite GOP's "razor-thin, historically small majority"
item.description: The House approved a Senate bill to fund the Department of Homeland Security on Thursday, signaling an end to the longest partial government shutdown in U.S. history. CBS News' Nicole Sganga, Ed O'Keefe and Nikole Killion have the latest.

item.title: House approves Senate bill to fund DHS and end 76-day shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday unanimously approved a Senate-passed bill to reopen the Department of Homeland Security, sending it to the president's desk.

item.title: Watch: GOP Sen. Ernst criticizes Hegseth for firing Army chief of staff
item.description: GOP Sen. Joni Ernst of Iowa criticized Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth for his decision to fire Army Chief of Staff Randy A. George. Hegseth appeared before a Senate committee to discuss the Pentagon's 2027 budget request.

item.title: Watch: Sen. Blumenthal grills Pentagon officials on cost of Iran war
item.description: Sen. Richard Blumenthal, a Connecticut Democrat, focused his line of questioning on the cost of the Iran war. Blumenthal said he believes the $25 billion cost of the war was less than the actual total.

item.title: Jewish Londoners describe "utter horror" and living in fear after latest attack
item.description: "We've been warning about this for a long time," one local resident told CBS News. "It's like a tsunami — you see the smaller waves before the big one hits."

item.title: Camp Mystic withdraws application for 2026 camp license, will not reopen
item.description: This comes on the heels of the first public hearing over last summer's Fourth of July floods that killed more than 100 people across the Hill Country, including 27 girls at Camp Mystic.

item.title: Thermos recalls 8.2 million containers after stoppers caused vision loss
item.description: The Thermos Stainless King Food Jars and Thermos Sportsman Food & Beverage Bottles were sold at Walmart, Target and Amazon.com.

item.title: How much interest can a 3-month CD account earn this May?
item.description: A 3-month CD comes with a high interest rate now. But that's not the only advantage to opening an account this May.

item.title: Watch: Gillibrand tells Hegseth "you don't care" whether Americans support Iran war
item.description: Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand of New York accused Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth of brushing off Americans' lack of support for the Iran war. "There's no evidence that we're safer because of this war," Gillibrand says.

item.title: How much will a $100,000 annuity pay each month at age 60?
item.description: Buying a $100,000 annuity at age 60 could lock in monthly income for life, but how much will you pocket each month?

item.title: House passes Senate bill to fund DHS and end 76-day government shutdown
item.description: The House of Representatives has approved a Senate-passed bill to fund the Department of Homeland Security. Speaker Mike Johnson reacted to the vote on Thursday.

item.title: Trump pulls Casey Means' nomination as surgeon general, naming new pick
item.description: President Trump blamed Republican Sen. Bill Cassidy for Casey Mean's nomination stalling in the Senate.

item.title: Tune in: Artemis II astronauts join "CBS Mornings" for a special live hour Friday
item.description: A few weeks back from their trip around the moon, the Artemis crew joins CBS News for "Artemis II: A Celebration of Heroes," a town hall featuring questions from students, and special guests.

item.title: Fire aboard Navy destroyer USS Higgins, officials say
item.description: One of the officials told CBS News that the fire knocked out power and propulsion on the guided-missile destroyer, a mainstay of the Navy's forward presence in Asia.

item.title: Suspect in D.C. shooting held in "safe cell," agrees to remain jailed ahead of trial
item.description: Cole Allen, 31, is facing three charges related to the attack outside the White House Correspondents' Dinner, including attempting to assassinate President Trump.

item.title: Coast Guard operating in "crisis" as shutdown halts pay, strains missions overseas
item.description: The Coast Guard will run out of funding to pay personnel on May 1, with the first missed paychecks expected May 15.

item.title: Oil prices hit wartime peak, with Brent crude touching $126 a barrel
item.description: Brent crude surged past $126 a barrel early Thursday, while U.S. gasoline prices jumped to $4.30 a gallon.

item.title: D4vd killed Celeste Rivas Hernandez to "silence" her, prosecutors allege
item.description: The singer is accused of killing 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

item.title: 3/22: Face the Nation
item.description: This week in "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," amid an escalation of the Iranian war, a new CBS News poll shows the conflict and rising gas prices are fueling economic concerns in the U.S. Plus, U.N. Ambassador Mike Waltz and NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte join.
